title: This Week in Beaufort County
subject: Port Royal Sounder No. 2: this week's events and weekly specials
preheader: A full 2026-03-16 to 2026-03-22 roundup, plus the weekly specials signals worth checking first.
intro: This issue is built from the live calendar plus the current Weekly Specials board. It includes everything currently on the calendar for the week, along with the strongest recurring specials and market signals we have compiled so far.

items.name: Spring Architects’ Tour
items.note: Saturday, March 21 at 10:00 AM. Historic Beaufort Foundation is listing What: 2026 Spring Architects’ Tour When: Saturday, March 21, 2026 Time: 10AM to 4PM Purchase Tickets Join Historic Beaufort Foundation for the much anticipated Spring Architects’ Tour as gracious owners open their private homes in support of HBF’s mission to preserve and protect Beaufort’s historic and architectural heritage. The annual Spring Architects’ tour is the Lowcountry’s only tour presenting the best in innovative and distinctive contemporary residential architecture and offers a rare opportunity to peek inside these homes as architects, builders, designers and owners share their expertise. HBF is pleased to feature 5 distinctive homes located in Beaufort’s Downtown Historic District, on Dataw Island and on St. Helena Island. This is primarily a self-driven tour. Golf carts and/or trolley service will be available in certain locations. Also, a part of the tour this year, the Dataw Historic Foundation will provide tours of their History Museum and Learning Center, and the Sams Plantation Ruins. This year’s tour features homes designed by three of Beaufort’s acclaimed architectural firms —Frederick & Frederick Architects; Hefner Residential Architecture, and Montgomery Planning & Architecture.

items.name: Ghost Parrots Night
items.note: Saturday, March 21 at 7:00 PM. Savannah Ghost Pirates is listing Home Ghost Pirates game against Greenville with the Ghost Parrots Night promotion.

items.name: Galactic Bowling
items.note: Saturday, March 21 at 8:00 PM. Station 300 Bluffton is listing Saturday night galactic bowling at Station 300.

items.name: Healthcare Heroes Appreciation

items.name: Port Royal Farmers Market
items.note: Reliable Saturday stop for produce, seafood, meat, cheese, flowers, honey, and bread at Naval Heritage Park.

items.name: Downtown Beaufort Farmers' Market
items.note: Good recurring pick for local produce and prepared foods right in the middle of downtown Beaufort.

items.name: Farmers' Market of Bluffton
items.note: Weekly Bluffton market with farms, specialty vendors, prepared foods, and a strong local-maker mix.

items.name: The Kitchen
items.note: A strong pick for grab-and-go meals, baked goods, coffee, catering, and pantry extras.

items.name: Lowcountry Produce Market & Cafe - Beaufort
items.note: Downtown go-to for breakfast and lunch plus bakery items, pantry goods, and prepared foods.

items.name: Butcher's Market & Deli
items.note: Worth checking this week for steaks, house-made sausage, deli picks, and easy take-home meals.

items.name: Time To Eat
items.note: A good weekly watch for burgers, cheesesteaks, seafood, and rotating specials around Beaufort pop-ups.

items.name: Lowco Grub
items.note: Often worth checking for comfort-food plates and brewery pop-ups around Bluffton.

items.name: Palmetto Pops
items.note: Seasonal frozen-treat option that shows up at family-friendly markets and events.

items.name: Shellring Ale Works
items.note: One of the easiest recurring music checks in Port Royal, especially if you want a casual beer-garden setting.

items.name: Port Royal Street Music
items.note: Seasonal street-series pick to keep on the radar for a walkable outdoor music night in town.

items.name: USCB Center for the Arts
items.note: Best weekly watch for ticketed concerts, touring performances, and more formal arts programming.

buttondownLastSubject: Port Royal Sounder No. 2: this week's events and weekly specials (Updated)

title: Lowcountry Weekend Starter
subject: Port Royal Sounder No. 1: markets, oysters, and a few local favorites
preheader: A first pass at the kind of useful local roundup this newsletter can become.
intro: This first issue is a compiled draft built from the directory and calendar sources already in Port Royal Sounder. The idea is simple: a small handful of places to try, events worth catching, and a short list of sources we think are worth watching each week.

items.name: The Kitchen
items.note: A strong prepared-food stop for grab-and-go meals, coffee, baked goods, catering, and pantry extras.

items.name: Herban Market & Cafe
items.note: A scratch-made market cafe with breads, pastries, fresh food, and a natural-products feel that fits the local roundup well.

items.name: Cypress Wetlands
items.note: One of the easiest local walks for birding, especially when you want something beautiful and low-effort close to town.

items.name: Port Royal Farmers Market
items.note: The anchor weekly market in this project, with local produce, seafood, meat, cheese, honey, flowers, and bread.

items.name: Downtown Beaufort Farmers' Market
items.note: A useful downtown stop for produce and prepared foods if you want something central and easy to combine with errands.

items.name: Farmers' Market of Bluffton
items.note: Worth the drive when you want a larger Thursday mix of local farms, makers, and specialty vendors.

items.name: 2026 FFF Oyster Roast
items.note: Friday, March 13 at Live Oaks Park. A classic Port Royal-style community event and an easy first pick for the calendar.

items.name: Radiance Run 5K
items.note: Saturday, March 14. A local community race that fits the kind of civic and neighborhood event we want to surface.

items.name: Third Thursday - Historic Port Royal Foundation
items.note: Thursday, March 19. Exactly the kind of recurring local event that deserves a reliable home in both the calendar and newsletter.

items.name: 4th Annual Spring Symposium
items.note: March 20 and 21 at USCB Center for the Arts, with a more academic and cultural angle than the usual weekend roundup.

items.name: Town of Port Royal Calendar
items.note: One of the most useful official feeds for town events, meetings, and community announcements.

items.name: Visit Beaufort Events
items.note: Useful for larger public events and tourism-facing happenings that still matter for locals.

items.name: USCB Center for the Arts
items.note: A strong arts and performance source that should likely feed both the newsletter and calendar regularly.
